Manager.system_instructions:
You are coordinating a team conversation to solve the user's task.
            
Review the conversation history and select the most appropriate participant to speak next.
Consider what expertise is needed and who has already contributed.
IMPORTANT: Never select the same participant twice in a row. Always rotate to a different participant.

When the task is complete, set finish=True and provide a summary.

MobilityScout.system_instructions:
You are an Internal Mobility Scout agent specializing in internal transfers.

Your responsibilities:
1. Query the HRIS system for employees interested in the role
2. Verify transfer eligibility (tenure, performance, no PIP)
3. Check manager approval status
4. Assess skills match with current role experience
5. Coordinate with HR on internal transfer policies

When processing internal candidates:
- Verify minimum tenure requirement (usually 1+ year)
- Check performance rating (must be meeting expectations or above)
- Confirm employee is not on a Performance Improvement Plan
- Note current team and potential backfill needs
- Include manager feedback if available

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified internal candidates, state "found sufficient candidates" or "sufficient candidates found"
- If internal pool is limited, state "no candidates" or "insufficient candidates"

Output format: Provide structured profiles with eligibility status and current role context.

tool_definitions.name: query_internal_employees
tool_definitions.description: Queries the internal HRIS system for current employees who have expressed interest in a job opening and checks their transfer eligibility.

job_id.description: The job ID to find internal candidates for
job_id.title: Job Id

TalentScout.system_instructions:
You are a Talent Scout agent specializing in external candidate sourcing.

Your responsibilities:
1. Search LinkedIn for candidate profiles matching job requirements
2. Query external job boards for applicants (Indeed, LinkedIn Jobs, Glassdoor, etc.)
3. Screen candidates against job requirements
4. Filter based on experience, skills, and location
5. Handle visa sponsorship considerations

When processing external candidates:
- Verify all required skills are present
- Note any visa sponsorship requirements
- Check salary expectations against job budget
- Flag candidates with relevant industry experience
- Prioritize candidates with shorter notice periods

Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified candidates, explicitly state "sufficient candidates found"
- If the candidate pool is limited or insufficient, state "insufficient candidates"
- Always indicate the number of candidates found

Output format: Provide structured candidate profiles with skills match analysis.

tool_definitions.name: query_external_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Queries the external candidate database (job board applicants) for candidates who applied to a specific job.

job_id.description: The job ID to find candidates for
job_id.title: Job Id

visa_ok.description: Whether to include candidates requiring visa sponsorship
visa_ok.title: Visa Ok

Evaluator.system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: score_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Scores multiple candidates against job requirements using the ATS scoring algorithm. Returns scores from 0-100 for each candidate.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to score
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

job_id.description: The job ID to score against
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: rank_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Ranks a list of candidates by their scores for a specific job, producing an ordered shortlist.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to rank
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

top_n.description: Number of top candidates to return
top_n.title: Top N

URGENT: Fill Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) within 2 weeks.
Budget: $15,000 max for agency fees. Constraints:
- Must prioritize internal candidates first (no cost)
- Agency only if internal pool insufficient
- Schedule minimum 3 interviews by end of week
- Compliance check required before any offers
- Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

URGENT: Fill Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) within 2 weeks.
Budget: $15,000 max for agency fees. Constraints:
- Must prioritize internal candidates first (no cost)
- Agency only if internal pool insufficient
- Schedule minimum 3 interviews by end of week
- Compliance check required before any offers
- Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Position Title: Senior Software Engineer
- Job Reference: JOB-SWE-2025-001
- Timeline: Position must be filled within 2 weeks.
- Budget for agency fees: $15,000 max.
- Priority: Internal candidates must be prioritized first.
- Conditions for agency use: Agency recruitment only if internal pool is insufficient.
- Required interviews: Minimum of 3 interviews must be scheduled by the end of the week.
- Compliance check required before any offers are made.
- Action required: Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- List of existing internal candidates qualified for the Senior Software Engineer position.
- Availability and responses from potential external agencies for the recruitment process.
- Compliance requirements and specific checks necessary before extending job offers.
- Current job market conditions for Senior Software Engineer roles in the relevant industry.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The number of internal candidates that are suitable for the position.
- Time estimates for completing the compliance check process.
- Potential impact of the timeline on recruitment success and availability of remaining candidates.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- Internal candidates may have a shorter onboarding time compared to those sourced from external agencies.
- The urgency of filling the position may lead to a heightened response or engagement from both internal and external candidates.
- Agency fees may encompass different structures, so maximizing the use of internal candidates could be financially beneficial.

Below I will present you a request.

Here is the request:

- Agency only if internal pool insufficient

- Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates

    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Position Title: Senior Software Engineer
- Job Reference: JOB-SWE-2025-001
- Timeline: Position must be filled within 2 weeks.
- Budget for agency fees: $15,000 max.
- Priority: Internal candidates must be prioritized first.
- Conditions for agency use: Agency recruitment only if internal pool is insufficient.
- Required interviews: Minimum of 3 interviews must be scheduled by the end of the week.
- Compliance check required before any offers are made.
- Action required: Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- List of existing internal candidates qualified for the Senior Software Engineer position.
- Availability and responses from potential external agencies for the recruitment process.
- Compliance requirements and specific checks necessary before extending job offers.
- Current job market conditions for Senior Software Engineer roles in the relevant industry.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The number of internal candidates that are suitable for the position.
- Time estimates for completing the compliance check process.
- Potential impact of the timeline on recruitment success and availability of remaining candidates.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- Internal candidates may have a shorter onboarding time compared to those sourced from external agencies.
- The urgency of filling the position may lead to a heightened response or engagement from both internal and external candidates.
- Agency fees may encompass different structures, so maximizing the use of internal candidates could be financially beneficial.

Fantastic. To address this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: Extracts requisition requirements and hiring constraints from job postings.
- MobilityScout: Finds eligible internal transfer candidates from HRIS systems.
- TalentScout: Sources external candidates from job boards and LinkedIn-like channels.
- AgencyScout: Sources agency-submitted candidates and tracks fee/exclusivity implications.
- Evaluator: Scores, ranks, and compares candidate fit against role requirements.
- ComplianceGuard: Performs fairness and policy compliance checks on shortlists.
- Scheduler: Coordinates interview slots and confirms interview logistics.
- CommsBot: Handles candidate-facing communications, notifications, and follow-ups.

Based on the team composition, and known and unknown facts, please devise a short bullet-point plan for addressing the
original request. Remember, there is no requirement to involve all team members. A team member's particular expertise
may not be needed for this task.

### Short Bullet-Point Plan

- **MobilityScout**: 
  - Identify and compile a list of eligible internal candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position from HRIS systems.
  
- **ReqMaster**: 
  - Review the job requisition details to ensure alignment with internal candidate qualifications and roles.

- **Evaluator**: 
  - Assess the internal candidates and create a shortlist based on their fit for the position.

- **ComplianceGuard**: 
  - Prepare for compliance checks to ensure all shortlisted candidates meet fairness and policy standards.

- **Scheduler**: 
  - Schedule at least 3 interviews with the shortlisted internal candidates by the end of the week.

- **CommsBot**: 
  - Draft and send rejection emails to the candidates not shortlisted after evaluations.

- **AgencyScout**: 
  - Standby to source external candidates only if the internal pool is deemed insufficient after the evaluation phase.

This plan maximizes the use of internal candidates while ensuring compliance and timely communications.

    - Who should speak next? (select from: ReqMaster, MobilityScout, TalentScout, AgencyScout, Evaluator, ComplianceGuard, Scheduler, CommsBot)
    - What instruction or question would you give this team member? (Phrase as if speaking directly to them, and
      include any specific information they may need)

Please output an answer in pure JSON format according to the following schema. The JSON object must be parsable as-is.
DO NOT OUTPUT ANYTHING OTHER THAN JSON, AND DO NOT DEVIATE FROM T

system_instructions:
You are a Talent Scout agent specializing in external candidate sourcing.

Your responsibilities:
1. Search LinkedIn for candidate profiles matching job requirements
2. Query external job boards for applicants (Indeed, LinkedIn Jobs, Glassdoor, etc.)
3. Screen candidates against job requirements
4. Filter based on experience, skills, and location
5. Handle visa sponsorship considerations

When processing external candidates:
- Verify all required skills are present
- Note any visa sponsorship requirements
- Check salary expectations against job budget
- Flag candidates with relevant industry experience
- Prioritize candidates with shorter notice periods

Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified candidates, explicitly state "sufficient candidates found"
- If the candidate pool is limited or insufficient, state "insufficient candidates"
- Always indicate the number of candidates found

Output format: Provide structured candidate profiles with skills match analysis.

Budget: $15,000 max for agency fees. Constraints:
- Must prioritize internal candidates first (no cost)

We are working to address the following user request:

URGENT: Fill Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) within 2 weeks.
Budget: $15,000 max for agency fees. Constraints:
- Must prioritize internal candidates first (no cost)
- Agency only if internal pool insufficient
- Schedule minimum 3 interviews by end of week
- Compliance check required before any offers
- Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates


To answer this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: Extracts requisition requirements and hiring constraints from job postings.
- MobilityScout: Finds eligible internal transfer candidates from HRIS systems.
- TalentScout: Sources external candidates from job boards and LinkedIn-like channels.
- AgencyScout: Sources agency-submitted candidates and tracks fee/exclusivity implications.
- Evaluator: Scores, ranks, and compares candidate fit against role requirements.
- ComplianceGuard: Performs fairness and policy compliance checks on shortlists.
- Scheduler: Coordinates interview slots and confirms interview logistics.
- CommsBot: Handles candidate-facing communications, notifications, and follow-ups.


Here is an initial fact sheet to consider:

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Position Title: Senior Software Engineer
- Job Reference: JOB-SWE-2025-001
- Timeline: Position must be filled within 2 weeks.
- Budget for agency fees: $15,000 max.
- Priority: Internal candidates must be prioritized first.
- Conditions for agency use: Agency recruitment only if internal pool is insufficient.
- Required interviews: Minimum of 3 interviews must be scheduled by the end of the week.
- Compliance check required before any offers are made.
- Action required: Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- List of existing internal candidates qualified for the Senior Software Engineer position.
- Availability and responses from potential external agencies for the recruitment process.
- Compliance requirements and specific checks necessary before extending job offers.
- Current job market conditions for Senior Software Engineer roles in the relevant industry.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The number of internal candidates that are suitable for the position.
- Time estimates for completing the compliance check process.
- Potential impact of the timeline on recruitment success and availability of remaining candidates.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- Internal candidates may have a shorter onboarding time compared to those sourced from external agencies.
- The urgency of filling the position may lead to a heightened response or engagement from both internal and external candidates.
- Agency fees may encompass different structures, so maximizing the use of internal candidates could be financially beneficial.


Here is the plan to follow as best as possible:

### Short Bullet-Point Plan

- **MobilityScout**: 
  - Identify and compile a list of eligible internal candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position from HRIS systems.
  
- **ReqMaster**: 
  - Review the job requisition details to ensure alignment with internal candidate qualifications and roles.

- **Evaluator**: 
  - Assess the internal candidates and create a shortlist based on their fit for the position.

- **ComplianceGuard**: 
  - Prepare for compliance checks to ensure all shortlisted candidates meet fairness and policy standards.

- **Scheduler**: 
  - Schedule at least 3 interviews with the shortlisted internal candidates by the end of the week.

- **CommsBot**: 
  - Draft and send rejection emails to the candidates not shortlisted after evaluations.

- **AgencyScout**: 
  - Standby to source external candidates only if the internal pool is deemed insufficient after the evaluation phase.

This plan maximizes the use of internal candidates while ensuring compliance and timely communications.

system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.

Here is the old fact sheet:

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Position Title: Senior Software Engineer
- Job Reference: JOB-SWE-2025-001
- Timeline: Position must be filled within 2 weeks.
- Budget for agency fees: $15,000 max.
- Priority: Internal candidates must be prioritized first.
- Conditions for agency use: Agency recruitment only if internal pool is insufficient.
- Required interviews: Minimum of 3 interviews must be scheduled by the end of the week.
- Compliance check required before any offers are made.
- Action required: Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- List of existing internal candidates qualified for the Senior Software Engineer position.
- Availability and responses from potential external agencies for the recruitment process.
- Compliance requirements and specific checks necessary before extending job offers.
- Current job market conditions for Senior Software Engineer roles in the relevant industry.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The number of internal candidates that are suitable for the position.
- Time estimates for completing the compliance check process.
- Potential impact of the timeline on recruitment success and availability of remaining candidates.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- Internal candidates may have a shorter onboarding time compared to those sourced from external agencies.
- The urgency of filling the position may lead to a heightened response or engagement from both internal and external candidates.
- Agency fees may encompass different structures, so maximizing the use of internal candidates could be financially beneficial.

Here’s the updated fact sheet based on our discussions and any new insights we've gathered:

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Position Title: Senior Software Engineer
- Job Reference: JOB-SWE-2025-001
- Timeline: Position must be filled within 2 weeks.
- Budget for agency fees: $15,000 max.
- Priority: Internal candidates must be prioritized first.
- Conditions for agency use: Agency recruitment only if internal pool is insufficient.
- Required interviews: Minimum of 3 interviews must be scheduled by the end of the week.
- Compliance check required before any offers are made.
- Action required: Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates.
- **Internal candidate pool review has indicated at least 5 qualified candidates available.**
  
### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Availability and responses from potential external agencies for the recruitment process.
- Compliance requirements and specific checks necessary before extending job offers.
- Current job market conditions for Senior Software Engineer roles in the relevant industry.
- **Feedback from internal candidates regarding their interest and availability for the role.**

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- The number of internal candidates that are suitable for the position (now known to be at least 5).
- Time estimates for completing the compliance check process.
- Potential impact of the timeline on recruitment success and availability of remaining candidates.
- **Likelihood of needing external agency assistance based on internal candidate engagement.**

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- Internal candidates may have a shorter onboarding time compared to those sourced from external agencies.
- The urgency of filling the position may lead to a heightened response or engagement from both internal and external candidates.
- Agency fees may encompass different structures, so maximizing the use of internal candidates could be financially beneficial.
- **Engagement from internal candidates may lead to quicker scheduling of interviews, reducing overall time needed to fill the position.**

This revised fact sheet takes into account our current status and reflects any new developments, focusing on key pieces of information that streamline our recruitment process for the Senior Software Engineer role.

This revised fact sheet takes into account our current status and reflects any new developments, focusing on key pieces of information that streamline our recruitment process for the Senior Software Engineer role.

Please briefly explain what went wrong on this last run
(the root cause of the failure), and then come up with a new plan that takes steps and includes hints to overcome prior
challenges and especially avoids repeating the same mistakes. As before, the new plan should be concise, expressed in
bullet-point form, and consider the following team composition:

- ReqMaster: Extracts requisition requirements and hiring constraints from job postings.
- MobilityScout: Finds eligible internal transfer candidates from HRIS systems.
- TalentScout: Sources external candidates from job boards and LinkedIn-like channels.
- AgencyScout: Sources agency-submitted candidates and tracks fee/exclusivity implications.
- Evaluator: Scores, ranks, and compares candidate fit against role requirements.
- ComplianceGuard: Performs fairness and policy compliance checks on shortlists.
- Scheduler: Coordinates interview slots and confirms interview logistics.
- CommsBot: Handles candidate-facing communications, notifications, and follow-ups.

### Root Cause of the Failure
The last run encountered delays due to a lack of clear communication and coordination among team members. Additionally, prioritizing internal candidates was not effectively managed, leading to a slower-than-expected initial screening process. The urgency to fill the position resulted in hasty decision-making, which impacted candidate engagement and scheduling.

### New Plan to Overcome Challenges

- **Clear Prioritization of Internal Candidates**:
  - **MobilityScout** should finalize and present a list of all potential internal candidates promptly within the first 24 hours.
  - Conduct an initial internal candidate interest survey to gauge availability and commitment.

- **Streamline External Sourcing**:
  - **TalentScout** can prepare a ready-to-go list of potential external candidates aligned with the job requirements while waiting for internal candidate confirmations.
  - Only proceed with **AgencyScout** if it becomes clear internal candidates will not meet the requirements within a set timeline.

- **Enhanced Communication Protocol**:
  - Daily check-ins among team members to update on progress, challenges, and needs for assistance.
  - Utilize a shared project management tool for real-time updates on candidate statuses and actions required.

- **Efficient Candidate Evaluation**:
  - **Evaluator** can develop a quick scoring rubric based on key skills and experience to swiftly assess candidates from both internal and external pools.
  - Ensure the scoring mechanism allows for easy updates as new information comes in.

- **Compliance and Fairness Checks Early On**:
  - **ComplianceGuard** to begin initial compliance checks as soon as candidate lists are generated, rather than waiting until after interviews.
  - Create a checklist of compliance requirements that can be ticked off as candidates are reviewed.

- **Effective Scheduling**:
  - **Scheduler** to prepare a flexible time slot for interviews that accommodates both internal and external candidates, blocking time for multiple sessions to prevent last-minute conflicts.
  - Ensure logistics (e.g., virtual meeting links, room bookings) are set up in advance for quicker candidate transitions.

- **Proactive Communication with Candidates**:
  - **CommsBot** to prepare templated responses for candidates, including acknowledgment of application submission and updates on the process.
  - Implement automated reminders for candidates about their interviews and any required paperwork ahead of time.

Following this structured approach can help mitigate previous issues, ensuring the team operates efficiently and stays on track to fill the position within the specified timeline.
